engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is choosing to trust a finance broker with their loan; the page sells defined expertise via credentials (Australian Credit Licence), team profiles, service breakdown, and customer stories. That is 'sells defined expertise', the first rung.

goal.one_action: book a 15-min call with a broker

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page brokerage: home positioning + service breakdown + team credibility + video testimonials + contact, all funneling to a single Calendly booking.

mapped.observed_as: your story matters. (4 service category buttons + testimonial)
schedule a call with Laurie (Calendly embed)
Residential / Commercial / Car & Equipment / SMSF service descriptions
get in touch (phone/email/address, map, credit licence)

confidence.why: fetch_quality full; clear brokerage archetype with unambiguous trust signals and single Calendly conversion path.

gap_findings: Testimonials are video-only (YouTube tiles) with no verbatim text quotes — a video-testimonial section shape differs from the quote-based testimonials the platform assumes, though 'gallery/portfolio' + testimonials mostly covers it.
All conversion routed through a single-step Calendly embed with zero on-site lead form — per registry, this is a delegated form, not a new capability.
